title: Lesson 13 — Status and Pride

words.definition: to feel deep respect or admiration for
words.example: Students revered the old professor for decades.
A colleague held in the highest esteem by everyone.

words.definition: polite submission and respect toward others
words.example: He treated his elders with unfailing deference.

words.definition: obedient or attentive to an excessive, servile degree
words.example: An obsequious waiter hovered, angling for a bigger tip.

words.definition: excessively willing to serve or please others; submissive
words.example: A servile assistant who agreed with every word.

words.definition: to give exaggerated flattery or affection to gain favor
words.example: Reporters fawned over the celebrity's every remark.

words.definition: the feeling that someone or something is unworthy of respect
words.example: She eyed the cheap imitation with open disdain.

words.definition: the feeling that a person or thing is beneath consideration or worthless
words.example: He spoke of the rules with undisguised contempt.
A haughty nod was all the duchess offered the crowd.

words.definition: having an exaggerated sense of one's own importance
words.example: Too arrogant to ask for directions, he drove in circles.

words.definition: to behave as if superior; to patronize
words.example: He condescended to explain, slowly, as if to a child.

words.definition: behaving as though superior to others; haughtily contemptuous
words.example: A supercilious sneer greeted the newcomers.

story: The old family expected the whole town to [[revere|revere]] them and hold their name in [[esteem|esteem]]. Servants showed exaggerated [[deference|deference]]; the butler was [[obsequious|obsequious]] and the footmen frankly [[servile|servile]], quick to [[fawn|fawn]] over any guest with a title. The heiress met tradespeople with cold [[disdain|disdain]] and undisguised [[contempt|contempt]]. She was [[haughty|haughty]], [[arrogant|arrogant]], and forever ready to [[condescend|condescend]] to those she thought beneath her, wearing a [[supercilious|supercilious]] smile that made even her admirers wince.
